items.title: Several infrastructure projects put on backburner as ACT 'at peak construction'

children.content: Some ACT government infrastructure projects, including health, emergency services and sports facilities in North Gungahlin, will be deferred to save about $700 million over four years.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a navy suit jacket and glasses sits indoors speaking with his hands.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a navy suit jacket and glasses sits indoors speaking with his hands.

items.title: Even kids want gambling ads banned, so why won't the government do it?

children.content: The government's proposed crackdown on gambling ads doesn't go as far as most Australians want. When you run the numbers, it's a win for the betting industry.

image.altText: Hands holding a mobile phone displaying a gambling app

items.title: Knitters find key to serenity during 'party, party, party' snow season

children.content: As the NSW ski season gets underway, Jindabyne's Snowy Stitchers Social Club finds connection and mindfulness in needles and yarn.

image.altText: Rachelle Edwards, wearing a knitted beanie and gloves, standings in front of a snow field.

items.title: 'Cybertongue' could prevent 70 million tonnes of milk waste every year

children.content: About a sixth of the world's milk is wasted, over 150 million tonnes every year, but a Canberra company has come up with new technology that could drastically reduce that.

gallery.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

thumbnail.altText: A line of two-litre milk bottles in a processing facility.

items.title: Canberra man facing almost 70 sexual assault, violence charges

children.content: Police said multiple women contacted them in late 2025 and early 2026 to accuse the man of sexual assault while he was in relationships with them.

image.altText: A man with his hands cuffed behind his back is led away by a police officer.

items.title: Brumbies set unwanted records as winless Aussie finals run continues

children.content: The ACT Brumbies have been dumped out of the Super Rugby Pacific finals series after setting unwanted club records in Wellington on Friday.

image.altText: Rugby players with hands on hips, looking dejected

items.title: Canberra Liberals say alleged violent threat was a 'misunderstanding'

children.content: The Canberra Liberals have distanced themselves from former leader Leanne Castley's allegation that she was threatened with physical assault, saying the remark related to an "animated discussion" that was quickly resolved.

gallery.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

thumbnail.altText: Two politicians speak at a press conference.

items.title: Young couple told by agent that landlords would not rent to 'people like you'

children.content: Hopeful renters say negative stereotypes about young people are making it almost impossible to secure a rental on the Sunshine Coast.

thumbnail.altText: A young couple stand outside a house. The man wears tradie yellows. The woman wear's colour nurse's scrubs.

items.title: Why a property downturn would be a blessing for the RBA

children.content: With two Australian banks cutting fixed home loan rates, there is a ray of hope that the cycle of interest rate hikes has peaked and borrowing costs may be lower down the track — another sign the pace of economic change has moved into overdrive.

image.altText: a skyline with houses

items.title: ASX remains in the red, NAB sees next RBA move as down

children.content: The local share market is in the red but has clawed back some ground from its early lows. Artificial intelligence giant OpenAI files to become a public company in the US. Follow live.

image.altText: A man watches the information screens at the ASX

items.title: Family's 'worst nightmare' after purchasing home filled with defects

children.content: After a young family invests their life savings in their dream home, only to discover it is filled with defects, SA's consumer watchdog issues a public warning notice about the builder responsible and seven other traders.

gallery.altText: A woman and a man holding a young child in front of a house

thumbnail.altText: A woman and a man holding a young child in front of a house

items.title: Six of the most contentious parts of the NDIS bill

children.content: Politicians are presiding over three days of public hearings into a bill paving the way for the biggest ever cuts to the NDIS.

image.altText: A graphic illustration of a person in a wheelchair, an NDIS logo and a pair of scissors

items.title: Trump booed before NBA Finals clash as chaos ensues outside stadium

children.content: US President Donald Trump cops the vitriol of the Madison Square Garden crowd after causing chaos to attend the NBA Finals.

image.altText: People watch a big screen which has Donald Trump making a salute.

items.title: The bad blood between Poland and Ukraine left over from World War II

children.content: Poland is weighing whether to strip Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of its top honour for renaming an army unit after Ukrainian nationalist insurgents who massacred Poles in World War II.

gallery.altText: Veterans of the Ukrainian Insurgent Army standing, one holds a German MP40.
A veteran of the Ukrainian Insurgent Army marching in a rally organised by far right groups in 2016, holding a flag.

thumbnail.altText: Veterans of the Ukrainian Insurgent Army standing, one holds a German MP40.
A veteran of the Ukrainian Insurgent Army marching in a rally organised by far right groups in 2016, holding a flag.
People march on a street in Kiev, carrying a black and gold banner as well as yellow and blue flags.

items.title: New leader, old problem for WA Liberals as Zempilas flirts with One Nation

children.content: As Basil Zempilas flirts with One Nation, will the right-wing populist party drag the WA Liberals down again, or will it become a life raft on the way to a political resurrection?

image.altText: A close up of the torso of a man in a suit surrounded by people

items.title: 'I'm all in': Jacinta Allan brushes off leadership rumblings

children.content: The Victorian premier said she had no plans to step aside despite some Labor MPs becoming increasingly frustrated by her performance as leader and more worrying opinion polls.

image.altText: An image of Jacinta Allan.

items.title: On the edge of 30, I left Australia for the first time

children.content: As I approached the age of 30, I longed to travel outside of Australia, but there was always an excuse or obstacle. A chance to share my culture with the world finally got me there.

gallery.altText: Molly in front of some of her art

thumbnail.altText: Molly in front of some of her art

items.title: Idris Elba thinks audiences won't support a black James Bond

children.content: Idris Elba has quashed rumours he was ever in the running to play James Bond, saying audiences are unlikely to come along for the ride if Bond is played by a black actor.

image.altText: Idris Elba in a boat on a body of water, waving, wearing a dark t-shirt

items.title: The criticism Marc Fennell always gets for Stuff the British Stole

children.content: Two years in the making, the Walkley Award-winning journalist is back with the third season of his globe-trotting historical mystery series. He's treating it like it's the last.

image.altText: Marc looks to the left while standing around lush greenery.

items.title: Doctors call for mandatory food star ratings to stop health 'crisis'

children.content: Health bodies want star ratings on all packaged foods to help shoppers make healthier choices, but the grocery industry says the voluntary system works.

gallery.altText: A logo with four stars and the caption Health Star Rating on a food packet.

thumbnail.altText: A logo with four stars and the caption Health Star Rating on a food packet.

items.title: Woman accused of murdering terminally ill partner granted bail

children.content: The judge said new evidence showed the Kylie Truswell Mobbs's husband David Mobbs, who had motor neurone disease, was "resolute" in his desire to die.

image.altText: David Mobbs and Kylie Truswell-Mobbs stand arm in arm with the ocean in the background.

items.title: Residents shocked by severe storm as South West WA homes left flooded

children.content: An unexpected deluge has flooded homes and public facilities in the South West WA town of Harvey. The community was pelted with 40mm of rain in one hour.

image.altText: Flooding in a street in the town of Harvey

items.title: 'Violence brokers' are transforming organised crime in Sydney

children.content: One of the state's top police officers has promised feuding gangs bringing terror to Sydney's streets will not exist "by the time we finish" as police stare down a new era of disorganised crime.

image.altText: Hooded man police tape CCTV graphic

items.title: Judge blocks Trump's $142,000 H-1B skilled worker visa fee

children.content: The Trump administration's planned fee for the H-1B visa was announced to prevent foreign workers from taking American jobs.

items.title: Escaped devil spotted on suburban Gold Coast CCTV after week on loose

children.content: A two-year-old Tasmanian devil has been on the loose since last Tuesday when she leapt out of her enclosure at a Gold Coast wildlife park.

image.altText: Tasmanian devil found in cctv.

items.title: Politically connected company won $15m government contract despite 'red flags'

children.content: A cybersecurity company with financial red flags received a $15m state contract in the dying days of the Miles government.

items.title: Healthy people in hospitals are costing millions every day

children.content: As many medically well patients wait in public hospitals for a spot in aged care, their bodies begin to deteriorate at a rate experts believe is preventable.

image.altText: A bed in a resident's room in an aged care home.

items.title: 'Extremely traumatising': Modern slavery victims let down by police, courts

items.title: The Taiwan crisis nobody is watching

children.content: Taipei sits at the physical centre of the global AI economy but remains shut out of the diplomatic rooms where its sovereign rights are negotiated, often by others.

items.title: ICC chief prosecutor suspended after probe into sex abuse claims

children.content: The International Criminal Court's chief prosecutor, Karim Khan, has been suspended pending a vote by member states on his fate, following a probe into accusations of sexual harassment made against him.

items.title: Pope meets abuse victims in Spain, while some say he's not doing enough

children.content: The pontiff, who is on a week-long visit to Spain, listens to proposals to make safeguarding against abuse more effective in the church.

items.title: Xi and Kim pledge deeper cooperation between China and North Korea

children.content: China and North Korea should strengthen exchanges "in diplomacy, law enforcement, and the military"
Chinese leader Xi Jinping has suggested during a rare two-day visit to Pyongyang.

items.title: Four-time veterans to late bolters, here's your Socceroos pocket guide

children.content: Mat Ryan and Mathew Leckie are at a record-equalling fourth FIFA World Cup, while Cristian Volpato headlines a host of first-timers, after his late switch from Italy. Here's your complete guide to the Socceroos' World Cup squad.

articles.title: Eriksen 'doing well' after on-field collapse during Denmark game
